title: Domnul Dan merge la Washington
summary: The article critically analyzes Romanian President Nicușor Dan's decision to attend the inauguration of the Board of Peace (BOP) in Washington, arguing it represents subordination to Trump's authority. The author questions Romania's strategic positioning between the EU and the US, suggesting Dan may be sympathetic to MAGA ideology and risks distancing Romania from core European allies.
talking_points: The Board of Peace exceeds its UN mandate and makes Trump president-for-life with veto power, representing a form of vassalage that major European powers have rejected
Only small, peripheral, or EU-skeptic states like Bulgaria and Hungary joined BOP as founding members, while Germany, France, and the UK refused
Dan's real motivation is to gain Trump's goodwill and erase Romania's negative MAGA reputation after the 2024 election annulment
Romania risks alienating core EU allies (France, Germany, UK) by aligning with Trump's Washington alongside figures like Viktor Orbán
There are credible concerns that Dan may be sympathetic to MAGA ideology, based on his past rejection of Western contractualism and his courtship of AUR's nationalist electorate
author: Rodica Culcer
